typecheck.notes: Manual syntax verification with py_compile passed for all .py files in src/

blockingIssues.description: VAL-SPEC-001: Test builds n-gram cache from the SAME sequence it queries against (training-on-test). Acceptance rates are inflated because every n-gram was indexed during build_from_sequence(). Should use separate prompt context to build cache, then measure acceptance on different continuation text.
VAL-SPEC-002: Test only checks that EAGLE classes exist, but contract requires >= 60% acceptance rate measurement on real text. No actual acceptance measurement performed.
VAL-SPEC-003: Test uses theoretical speedup estimation formula instead of actual benchmark timing. Contract requires >= 30% speedup but test estimates from flawed acceptance measurement.

appliedUpdates.description: Added note about NgramCache _continuations list behavior - multiple matches possible per context, can result in >100% match rate if counting matches vs actual accepted tokens

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add boundary: Validation tests must measure what the validation contract asserts, not just check infrastructure exists. If an assertion specifies a performance threshold (e.g., '>= 50% acceptance'), the test must perform an actual measurement.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: VAL-SPEC-002 test passes with just class existence check but contract requires '>= 60% acceptance rate on real text'. Same pattern in VAL-SPEC-001/003 where estimation replaces measurement.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: kernel-optimization-worker skill should enforce: For features with performance targets in validation contract, validation tests MUST include actual benchmark timing measurements, not theoretical estimations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: VAL-SPEC-003 has '>= 30% speedup' target but test uses formula `speedup = 1 + acceptance * (avg_draft - 1)` instead of timing speculative_decode vs standard decode.
